title: Francophone Immigration: Central African Republic - Canada.ca
content:
## Francophone Immigration: Central African Republic

## Central African Republic

## French and English Speakers
Geographical distribution of the population

## Predominant sectors of activity
- Agriculture – Fisheries and related production
- Manufacturing industry
- Finance – Banking – Insurance
- Trade – Sales and services
- Information and communications technology
Distribution of sectors
Definition according to the World Bank: The total labour force includes people aged 15 and older who meet
                the International Labour Organization’s definition of the economically active population, that is, all
                individuals who provide labour for the production of goods and services during a given period. This
                definition includes both workers and job seekers. While national practices vary in the treatment of
                groups such as armed forces personnel and seasonal or part-time workers, in general, the labour force
                includes armed forces personnel, job seekers and first-time job seekers, but excludes homemakers and
                other caregivers and workers in the informal sector who are not paid.
Primary sector: all economic activities producing raw materials (agriculture, forestry,
                fisheries and, to some extent, mining)
Secondary sector comprises all economic activities corresponding to the transformation
                of raw materials into productive or consumer goods. (This is the industrial sector.)
Tertiary sector: sector of economic activities including trade, transport, services,
                etc.
Inexact data: In certain cases, the data provided may be imprecise or incomplete and may
                not add up to 100%.
The information contained in these pages is subject to change. Updates will be made as often as possible.
